saints.name: Martyr Savva Stratelates “the General” of Rome,  and 70 soldiers with him

troparion.text: You abandoned earthly rank and honor, / and as an athlete, confessed Christ God before the false and tyrannical emperors. / For His sake, you endured much suffering, O glorious one. / Therefore, splendidly adorned with the armies of heaven, / you have been crowned with a beautiful wreath of victory / by the King who reigns over all. / As you stand before Him, O Savva, / pray that He may save our souls.

kontakion.text: You were shown to be an invincible athlete, most blessed Savva, / overcoming the snares of the barbarians. / You contended with faithfulness, / conquering a multitude of the invisible enemy. / Therefore, you have woven a wreath of victory. / Most glorious one, entreat Christ for those who honor you with faith.

saints.name: Venerable Savva the Recluse of the Kiev Far Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Savva, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

saints.name: Venerable Alexius the Recluse of the Kiev Far Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Alexius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

saints.name: Martyrs Valentine and Pasikrates in Moesia, Bulgaria

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Valentine and Pasikrates, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

saints.name: Martyrs Eusebius, Neon, Leontius, Longinus, and others, at Nicomedia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

saints.name: Venerable Thomas the Fool of Syria

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Thomas, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

In you, O Mother, was preserved with exactness what is according to the image;  / for you took up the Cross and followed Christ.  / By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away;  / but to care for the soul, since it is immortal. /  Therefore, O Venerable Elizabeth, your spirit rejoices with the Angels.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “As the first fruits of our nature...”) 
As a temple of virginity and a treasury of virtues,  / you pour forth miracles abundantly, as an unfailing fountain of grace, O Mother Elizabeth,  / and you purge all sickness from the souls and bodies of those who sing devoutly to the Creator: “Alleluia.”

saints.name: Saint Iorest, Metropolitan of Ardeal, Confessor of Romania

troparion.text:
Confessors of Orthodoxy and good archpastors of the Church of Christ, /  you directed the people,
O blessed hierarchs and victory-bearers, Saints Iorest and Savva, / you who are worthy of the crown
of life, / pray the Lord to save our souls.

kontakion.text:
(Special Melody: “You have appeared today...“)
Most blessed Hierarchs of the Orthodox Faith,  /  and defenders of the Church of Christ,  / protect all Orthodox Christians who always sing:  /  Rejoice, Holy Hierarchs Iorest and Savva,  / wondrous Confessors of the Lord.

saints.name: Saint Savva Brancovici, Metropolitan of Ardeal, Confessor of Romania

troparion.text: Champion of Orthodoxy, teacher of purity and of true worship, / the enlightener of the universe and the adornment of hierarchs: / all-wise father Savva, your teachings have gleamed with light upon all things. / Intercede before Christ God to save our souls.

saints.name: Hieromartyr and Confessor Elijah (Ilie) the Wallachian

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Elijah, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God of Molcha

troparion.text: Today the glorious city of Moscow rejoices resplendently, /  and in Putivl there is a great celebration as they honor the Mother of God and her wondrous Icon,  / which permits everyone to quench their spiritual thirst with the honey of grace.

kontakion.text: Come, O people, let us run to our Sovereign Lady,  / for she has given us her Molcha Icon, which exudes streams of healing, /  granting all manner of help for spiritual and bodily illnesses,  / and leading to eternal life.

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Branko, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

saints.name: Saint Joseph the Confessor of Maramures

troparion.text: Great servant of God, Holy Hierarch Father Joseph,  / we honor and remember your name in songs.  / For by confessing Christ in Maramureş1 and defending the true Faith,  / you became the protector of your flock.  / Therefore, pray to Christ God to save our souls.

kontakion.text: You were revealed to your flock as a guide and most gentle teacher of the faithful,   / and serving with humility, you confessed Christ.   / Wherefore, with ceaseless praise we sing to you saying,   / “Rejoice, Holy Hierarch Father Joseph,   / protector of the Orthodox of Maramureş.”

saints.name: Hieromartyr Branko, parish priest of Veljusa

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Branko, entreat Christ God to save our souls.
